dependencies: Third-party data center providers for hosting services
Public cloud service providers (Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud)
Third-party hardware and software equipment for data center infrastructure
Global system integrators (Accenture, Cognizant, Deloitte, EY, KPMG)
Managed service providers and resale partners
risks: Reliance on third-party data center providers for service availability
Dependence on public cloud service providers for infrastructure
Supply chain attacks and third-party security incidents
Vulnerabilities in third-party or open-source software
Concentration of business with small number of third-party distributors for U.S. federal government sales
Third-party service provider failures or security breaches
Global supply chain disruptions from geopolitical events

regulatory_risks: Data privacy and security laws (EU Data Act, GDPR, Trans-Atlantic Data Privacy Framework)
AI and machine learning regulations (EU AI Act)
Export control laws and economic sanctions (Russia, Belarus, Iran sanctions)
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
Government procurement regulations and contracting requirements
Intellectual property laws and patent regulations
Tax laws and regulations (U.S. federal, state, international)
OECD Pillar 2 global minimum tax rules
Climate change regulations and reporting requirements
Cybersecurity regulations and incident reporting requirements

sanction_risks: Economic and trade sanctions against Russia and Belarus due to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Potential wider economic and trade sanctions if conflicts continue or worsen
Export control restrictions on Russia and Belarus
Sanctions compliance risks in heavily-regulated industries
Potential sanctions on other countries if geopolitical conflicts arise
Impact on business operations in sanctioned countries
